title: Local development worker for console chat via dev SQS queue

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
## Architecture

Enable local development of the console chat feature by routing dev-targeted SQS messages to a separate per-developer queue instead of the production queue.

### Why separate queue (not ESM filter)

AWS Lambda EventSourceMapping filter criteria DELETES non-matching messages rather than leaving them for other consumers. So the per-developer queue approach is required — dev messages never enter the production queue.

### Routing flow

1. Frontend reads NEXT_PUBLIC_CONSOLE_WORKER_TARGET env var and adds workerTarget to clientInstrumentation
2. startConsoleRun Lambda reads workerTarget; routes to CONSOLE_RUN_DEV_QUEUE_URL_{TARGET} env var for non-production targets
3. Local worker (local_worker.py) long-polls the dev queue and processes messages with local Plexus code

### Queue

Dev queue: https://sqs.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/695039645615/plexus-console-run-dev-local-ryan
Lambda env var added: CONSOLE_RUN_DEV_QUEUE_URL_LOCAL_RYAN

### Files changed

- dashboard/amplify/data/resolvers/startConsoleRun.ts — queue routing by workerTarget
- dashboard/components/ui/conversation-viewer.tsx — pass workerTarget in instrumentation
- dashboard/amplify/functions/consoleRunWorker/local_worker.py — local polling script
- dashboard/.env.local — NEXT_PUBLIC_CONSOLE_WORKER_TARGET=local:ryan (not committed)

### Current status (2026-04-27)

Lambda redeployed with routing code. CONSOLE_RUN_DEV_QUEUE_URL_LOCAL_RYAN added to startConsoleRun Lambda env. Local worker running against dev queue. End-to-end verification pending.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Architecture pivot: replace explicit startConsoleRun/SQS/Task hot path with ChatMessage-as-command dispatch. Add responseTarget/responseStatus fields, ChatMessage stream Lambda for cloud-targeted messages, local worker for local:<developer> targets, and one shared hard-coded Tactus Console response harness reused by cloud and local execution.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Implemented the stream-dispatch architecture in the working tree. Summary: ChatMessage now carries responseTarget/responseStatus lifecycle fields; Console frontend creates only the USER ChatMessage with responseStatus=PENDING; cloud Docker Lambda is wired to the ChatMessage stream instead of SQS; local_worker.py polls local:<developer> pending messages; shared plexus.console.chat_runtime owns message filtering, conditional claim, hard-coded Tactus Console execution, and completion/failure marking. Verification: pytest plexus/console/test_chat_runtime.py -q passed; pytest dashboard/amplify/functions/consoleRunWorker/app_test.py plexus/console/test_chat_runtime.py -q passed; pytest plexus/cli/procedure/test_chat_recorder_account_resolution.py -q passed; pytest plexus/cli/procedure/test_trace_sink.py -q passed; dashboard Jest streaming test passed; dashboard npm run typecheck passed; dashboard npm run build:fast passed. npm run lint could not run because the existing script calls next lint --no-cache and the installed Next CLI rejects --no-cache before linting.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Implemented stream-driven Console chat dispatch architecture. Post-rebase verification: Python focused suite 44 passed; Jest conversation streaming test 6 passed; dashboard typecheck passed; build:fast passed. npm run lint is blocked by existing Next CLI option error: next lint --no-cache unknown option.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Expanded programmatic verification before manual npm-run-dev testing. Added tests for local poller index query/pagination, harness failure marking, stream Lambda partial batch failure, missing stream NewImage, explicit Console session reuse, and built-in Tactus context handoff. Also tightened local worker polling to use the responseStatusCreatedAt sort-key condition instead of a post-query filter. Verification: focused Python suite 53 passed; dashboard Jest streaming suite 6 passed; dashboard typecheck passed; py_compile passed for app.py/local_worker.py/chat_runtime.py.
